_note: Standard chart-pattern catalog (common public TA taxonomy, e.g. Bulkowski / Investopedia). Injected into the TradingView flow prompt so the agent names patterns from a fixed vocabulary; the oracle checks emitted pattern names against this catalog for structural validity (not whether the pattern is genuinely present).

patterns.name: Double Top
patterns.definition: Two peaks at similar highs separated by a trough; bearish reversal once the trough (neckline) breaks.

patterns.name: Double Bottom
patterns.definition: Two troughs at similar lows separated by a peak; bullish reversal once the peak (neckline) breaks.

patterns.name: Triple Top
patterns.definition: Three peaks at similar highs; bearish reversal on support break.

patterns.name: Triple Bottom
patterns.definition: Three troughs at similar lows; bullish reversal on resistance break.

patterns.name: Head and Shoulders
patterns.definition: Three peaks, the middle (head) highest; bearish reversal on neckline break.

patterns.name: Inverse Head and Shoulders
patterns.definition: Three troughs, the middle (head) lowest; bullish reversal on neckline break.

patterns.name: Ascending Triangle
patterns.definition: Flat resistance with rising lows; typically resolves upward.

patterns.name: Descending Triangle
patterns.definition: Flat support with falling highs; typically resolves downward.

patterns.name: Symmetrical Triangle
patterns.definition: Converging trendlines (lower highs, higher lows); breakout in either direction.

patterns.name: Bull Flag
patterns.definition: Sharp rise then a small downward-sloping consolidation; bullish continuation.

patterns.name: Bear Flag
patterns.definition: Sharp drop then a small upward-sloping consolidation; bearish continuation.

patterns.name: Pennant
patterns.definition: Small symmetrical triangle after a strong move; continuation in the prior direction.

patterns.name: Rising Wedge
patterns.definition: Converging up-sloping lines; typically bearish resolution.

patterns.name: Falling Wedge
patterns.definition: Converging down-sloping lines; typically bullish resolution.

patterns.name: Cup and Handle
patterns.definition: Rounded bottom (cup) then a small pullback (handle); bullish continuation.

patterns.name: Ascending Channel
patterns.definition: Price between two parallel up-sloping trendlines (uptrend).

patterns.name: Descending Channel
patterns.definition: Price between two parallel down-sloping trendlines (downtrend).

patterns.name: Rectangle
patterns.definition: Sideways range between horizontal support and resistance; breakout either direction.
